title: AT Field sound effect
wikitext:
[]: A.T. Field sound effect             Quote Post by Lambdadelta » Wed Feb 20, 2013 6:53 am Welcome everybody :) I'm currently searching for a A.T. Field sound effect for my mobile (to make a fancy message sound ) but I can't find it anywhere (I even extracted the sound bank from the Evangelion 64 but still found nothing). Do you know where can I find it? PS: I'm talking about that sound that comes for example when Shinji accidentally "stabs" Kaworu with a prog knife in episode 24.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Feb 20, 2013 9:41 am Have you tried recording it directly from the series?

[]:             Quote Post by Lambdadelta » Wed Feb 20, 2013 11:51 am Yes, acually I was already thinking about that but everytime they are using A.T. Field you can hear music in the background which isn't good :( Or maybe do you recall a scene in the series when they are using A.T. Field without music in the background?

[]:             Quote Post by Hedgestar » Wed Feb 20, 2013 12:17 pm I believe the Death movie is on Youtube. Try to find a spot where recording the sound effect will be clear.

[]:             Quote Post by alexprevity » Thu Feb 28, 2013 9:33 pm Lambdadelta wrote: Yes, acually I was already thinking about that but everytime they are using A.T. Field you can hear music in the background which isn't good :( Or maybe do you recall a scene in the series when they are using A.T. Field without music in the background?

[]:             Quote Post by Tetsuo87 » Sat Mar 02, 2013 12:04 pm I had done similar for the entry plug sound: extracting audio track from multi-channel bluray then with pro tools I had mixed so as to create an audio track that lasts 10 minutes. but in theory can become an infinite loop. will post on youtube soon... and a link to the wav file

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Mar 02, 2013 1:36 pm Tetsuo87 wrote: I had done similar for the entry plug sound: extracting audio track from multi-channel bluray then with pro tools I had mixed so as to create an audio track that lasts 10 minutes. but in theory can become an infinite loop. will post on youtube soon... and a link to the wav file
